board_csa_architect.notes: Time-partitioned JSONB table is the right pattern for high-volume event storage. Monthly partitions with 90-day retention keeps size manageable. Indexes on (event_type, created_at) and (org_id, created_at) cover the primary query patterns. Mirrors audit_logs pattern.

board_ciso_cybersecurity.notes: PII boundary is well-defined. RLS policies enforce Org-0-only cross-tenant access. Anti-AC explicitly forbids prompt/chat/task content in telemetry. INSERT-only policy on telemetry table is appropriate.

board_caio_fullstack.notes: No AI/VRAM impact. Telemetry is passive metadata collection. Async write path ensures no inference latency impact. Batch flush at 500ms is conservative enough.

board_vp_ops_devops.notes: Monthly partitioning + 90-day retention prevents unbounded growth. Async ingestion means telemetry failure doesnt crash the app. Dead-letter to stderr is appropriate for v1.

board_red_team.notes: Conditions: (1) payload JSONB must be validated server-side — reject if >50KB per event to prevent storage abuse. (2) Rate-limit INSERT to 1000 events/min per org to prevent telemetry flooding DoS. (3) Add a censor utility wrapper on payload before INSERT to catch accidental PII leaks. Failure scenarios: unbounded JSONB growth if no size check, telemetry flooding from compromised tenant, accidental prompt content leak via poorly-structured tool event.
